title: Level 264
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Aleo:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&display=swap'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Times New Roman'; }} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.5); /* Dark Blue */ --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 50, 0); --theme-link-color: rgba(150, 150, 150,1); /* Blue */ --theme-link-color--hover: #3333CC; /* Darker Blue for Hover */ --theme-link-label-color: #000; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(30, 30, 60, 0.4); /* Light Blue */ --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(40, 40, 70, 0.4); /* Lighter Blue */ --theme-link-color: rgba(30, 30, 255, 1); /* Light Blue for Links */ --theme-link-color--hover: #6666FF; /* Light Blue for Hover */ --theme-link-label-color: #000; } }}
Level 264 {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 264: "Translucent Paths"}} {{Blurbackground|file=Level_246_background.png}} {{Class 2e|Hexagon=2E}} Level '''264''' is the ''265th'' level of The Backrooms.

'''Description'''

{{Underline|808080}} {{Blurimage|width=400px|align=Right|file=Level_264_first_phot.png|caption=The First Known Image of Level 264|hovertext=First Image Ever Taken on Level 264|bg-size=}}
'''Level 264''', known as '''"Translucent Paths,"''' is a seemingly infinite expanse of mountainous terrains connected by bridges with a resistant, translucent glass floor. The glass bridge features protective fences made out of a similar glass to the ones on the floor of the bridges, but transparent rather than translucent. Some of the glass panels in the level appear more fragile than others and could collapse upon contact. The mountains in the level have a rough surface similar to those in The Frontrooms, but they are not made of normal stone. The mountains at the level appear to have a strange, geometric structure, and some have been reported to be floating in mid-air. The architecture of these bridges is extremely similar to those of [[The Frontrooms]], with a few differences, as some of them are weakened; even so, the bridges are very stable and resistant. Although the glass bridge is resistant, the glass fence is highly fragile, as any contact with it causes the fence to shatter and collapse. Even though '''Level 264''''s winds are strong, they cannot damage the fence. {{Blurimage|width=350px|align=Left|file=Level_264-b.png|caption=Another Image from Level 264|hovertext=Another Image on Level 264}} '''Level 264''' has perennially cloudy weather, with rare cases of rain. The level has a day-night cycle where each cycle takes about 12 hours, with 6 hours of daylight and 6 hours of nighttime. At night, the fog at the bridges and the mountains thickens to low lying, which appears to tranquilize panic-stricken wanderers. The temperature in Level 264 generally remains cool from 62-68 °F (17-20 °C). '''Level 264''' has winds of about 1mph (1.6 kmh). A constant cold breeze blows through the level, though some spots may feel warmer or cooler. This breeze gives wanderers a sense of safety and creates the illusion that the level is ''completely'' devoid of danger. The mechanism behind these phenomena is unknown. The mountains on '''Level 264''' are said to be about 20–100 million miles in height, and some seem to go on forever. Climbing even the lowest part of these mountains is deemed impossible due to their steepness. The ground of the level is the safest area throughout the entire level, where crates can be found with useful supplies inside.

'''The Dawn Staircase'''

{{Underline|808080}}{{Class Ψ|entity=High Mental Hazards|hexagon=Ψ|class=Ψ}}{{Blurimage|width=350px|align=right|file=dawncase264.png|caption=A photo of '''The Dawn Staircase'''.|top=}} '''The''' '''Dawn Staircase''' is the second and most dangerous section in '''Level 264'''. It consists of a poor-quality wooden staircase that can appear at the end of brick hallways with doors within the mountains on '''Level 264;''' these hallways are the only way to get into the area. The hallways are normally found on the edges of mountains, where the staircase goes down the slope to the bottom. This sub-layer is extremely dangerous; wanderers often report feeling suffocating paranoia and massive headaches, which makes it almost impossible to descend the stairs. Some of the steps are missing, damaged, or fully broken, but the stair rail is the only thing that stays in one piece. Because of how awful the steps are, some parts of the staircase can only be navigated by using the rail, but this can be almost impossible due to the mental strain. '''The''' '''Dawn Staircase''' is always in a dawntime period, which makes it even harder to see and go down. A wanderer's ultimate goal within the section is to descend the stairs completely; doing so will make one get to the next section: '''The Ground'''. The hallways formed to be the entrance into the area are normally made up of brick walls and gray tiled floors with a wooden door on the end. These corridors are dimly lit, with 2 or 3 lights. This part of the sub-layer is usually 78-89 feet (23 to 27 meters) in length, 7 feet (2 meters) in height, and 6-11 feet (1,8 to 3 meters) wide. Additionally, the light within the halls may flicker, which wanderers report feeling unsecure from. The section is also said to be extremely hot, ranging from 96 to 107 Fº (36 to 42 Cº). Upon entry into the section, the time and weather instantly switch to a foggy dawn. One will find huge trouble while trying to descend the stairs in the sub-layer due to the earlier mentioned steepness of the mountains, the mental hazards, the poor quality of the stairs, etc.; even so, this is the only confirmed and known way of reaching '''The Ground''' sub-layer, the last and safest area within '''Level 264'''.

'''The Ground'''

{{Underline|808080}} {{Class Habitable}} {{Blurimage|width=400px|align=left|file=Level_264_The_Ground.png|caption=A picture of '''The Ground''' sub-layer, taken on a rare time where it is day.}} '''The Ground''' is the 3rd and safest section of '''Level 264'''. As the name suggests, it is the ground of '''Level 264''', where you can see the base of the mountains. The area is thought to be infinite, just like the main part of '''Level 264'''. This sub-layer can only be accessed through '''The Dawn Staircase'''. '''The Ground''' is completely devoid of any harm in any form or shape. The section has crates with useful supplies scattered around it, which also seem to appear at random occasionally. Trees are common within the area, and most contain an abundance of fruits. Lakes/ponds made up of condensed almond water can be found throughout the area; the lakes can be 13-23 feet wide (3-7 meters) and 5-10 feet deep (1-10 meters). A strange phenomenon regarding the lakes is that you can't drown in them, and instead, the level teleports you back to the lake's shore. The section is most of the time at night, with rare occurrences of daylight. The low-density fog of '''Level 264''' mentioned earlier is also present here; it has the same effects, adding to the sublayer's safety. In '''The Ground''', one may feel an occasional cold breeze, just like in the glass bridges of '''Level 264''''s main area. This breeze gives wanderers a feeling of wanting to stay in the level, together with a vivid sense of safety. The moon in this section is always in the middle of the sky, even at day. '''The Ground''' sub-layer is very cold, 15 to 18 Fº (-9 to -7 Cº), which can sometimes allow the area to snow. Just like in [[The Frontrooms]], this area has seasons: winter, summer, spring, and autumn. This affects most of the area, including the grass, the trees, the temperature, etc. This adds to the effect of holding onto the level because of how similar it looks to [[The Frontrooms]]. '''The Ground''' section presents a weird phenomenon that doesn't happen in any other section of '''Level 264; t'''he phenomenon consists of showing a false ''reality,'' where the mountains geometric structures are normal and look like the ones from [[The Frontrooms]], which contradicts what's shown on other parts of '''Level 264''', where the mountains seem to be randomized, floating in the air, no-clipping into eachother, etc. As mentioned beforehand, one can find multiple crates lying around within '''The Ground''' section; here is a list of what items one could find in such crates. {{Blurimage|caption=A shot of two crates filled with tomatoes.|file=Img264ground.png|align=Top|width=400px}} {| class="article-table" ! style="background-color: #d3d3d3;color: black" |Items commonly found in crates on '''The Ground''' sub-layer. ! style="background-color: #d3d3d3;color: black" |Rarity of each item. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |[[Object 1|Almond Water]]. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |12.5%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |[[Object 15|Firesalt]]. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |3.7%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Hammers. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |15.8%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Wood planks. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |14.3%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Metal. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |11.5%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Cardboard. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |18.9%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Nails. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |26.7%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Knifes. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |7.1%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Fruits and other types of food. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |29.3%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |Water bottles. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |14.6%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |[[Object 2|Level Keys]]. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |1%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |[[Object 6|Moth Jelly]]. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |5.3%. |- | style="background-color: #ffffff;color: black" |[[Portable Purifiers]]. | style="background-color: #dadada;color: black" |1.2%. |- |}

'''Bases, Outposts and Communities'''

{{Underline|808080}}

'''Level 264''' '''and''' '''The Ground''' '''sub-layer are safe enough for bases and communities to be formed; even so, only 2 confirmed bases exist.'''

'''Helpers'''

*4 Members; *Helps new wanderers explore the level, sub-layers and it's exits; *Friendly to every wanderer, except enemy groups; *Stays on the main area of the Level.

'''M.E.G. 264 Exploration Base'''

*18 members; * Explores '''The Ground''' sub-layer; *Up to trade; *Has a giant base formed close to a mountain with a huge stock of supplies, on top of the entrance it says: "M.E.B. 264"; *Normally located on '''The Ground''', but some members stay in the main area, helping and giving out resources to new wanderers.

'''O.L.A.B. Base Sarı '''

*13 members; * Explores '''The Ground''' sub-layer; *Not up to trade; *Owns a small research outpost located on a mountain side.

'''Discovery'''

{{Underline|808080}} '''Level 264''' was discovered on May 25th, 1853, and it's sub-layers on April 10th, both by an anonymous wanderer who is deceived. The level has been further explored since then, but new discoveries are yet to be made, such as new entrances and exits. This wanderer took the photograph for '''The Dawn Staircase''' section.

'''Entrances and Exits'''

{{Underline|808080}}

'''Entrances'''

'''There's a few ways one could enter '''Level 264''', listed below.'''

*Finding an out-of-place glass staircase on [[Level 10]] and climbing it leads one here. *Walking on a glass bridge within [[Level 11]] will make it slowly fade into '''Level 264'''. *Grabbing a glass recipient on [[Level 75]] and smashing it against your own head has a small chance of leading you here. *No clipping into glass doors on [[Level List|any level]] has a small chance of leading one here.

'''Exits'''

'''There are a lot of ways one could exit '''Level 264''', listed below.'''

*Clipping through one of the mountains leads the individual to [[Level 8]]. *No-clipping through a broken step on T'''he Dawn Staircase''' sub-layer sends one to [[Level 293]]. *Occasional mono-yellow hallways lead to [[Level 0]]. *Trying to "skip" '''The Dawn Staircase''' sub-layer no-clips one to [[The Void|The Void.]] *Rarely, a glitch may happen within '''The Dawn Staircase''', sending one to [[Level 404]]. * Finding a beach in '''The Ground''' area no-clips one into [[Level 100]]. * Fire exit doors that can be opened oftenly sends one to [[Level 1]], [[Level 2|Level 2,]] or [[Level 3]]. *Finding a seemingly randomly placed washing machine sends one to [[Level 950]]. *Finding a hole filled with fog and going through it makes one wake up in [[Level 79]].
